rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. ELAINE H FLEMING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 21, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANT ELAINE FLEMING's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is off calendar. On April 10, 2013, the clerk entered default against the moving party, Defendant Elaine H. Fleming, an individual. "Entry of defendant's default instantaneously cuts off its right to appear in the action. The defendant is 'out of court.' [She] has no right to participate in the proceedings until either (a) its default is set aside (in which event, it may respond to the complaint), or (b) a default judgment is entered (in which event, [she] may appeal)." (Weil & Brown, Civil Procedure Before Trial 5:6.) Accordingly, Ms. Fleming's filing of the motion for summary judgment is improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Further, the Court, sua sponte, intends to reconsider its interim order issued on June 24, 2013 taking Ms. Fleming's motion to set aside default "off calendar as Plaintiff's request for default was rejected on May 22, 2013," and to consider the merits of Ms. Fleming's motion to set aside default. The interim order appears to have been in error. It appears that default had already been entered against Ms. Fleming on April 10, 2013. The Court will hold a hearing on November 22, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302 to reconsider its interim order of June 24, 2013. If they wish, the parties may file and serve briefs regarding the Court's sua sponte motion to reconsider its interim order of June 24, 2013. Ms. Fleming may file and serve a brief, no longer than 10 pages, no later than November 8, 2013 at 4 pm. Cach, LLC may file and serve a responsive brief, no longer than 10 pages, no later than November 15, 2013 at 4 pm. Courtesy copies of these papers, including proofs of service, shall be delivered and lodged with Department 302 on the date of filing. The parties shall also provide courtesy copies of all papers filed in support of and in opposition to Ms. Fleming's motion to set aside default that was originally noticed for hearing on June 24, 2013. Courtesy copies shall be delivered to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date of November 22, 2013. These papers shall be provided by November 8, 2013 at 4 pm. The Court will serve a copy of this order on the parties. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MERCHANTS EXCHANGE BUILDING LLC VS. SUNG CHO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 21, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT SUNG CHO's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Denied. Defendant's motion is untimely. Under CCP §473(b), a motion to set aside must be made within six months of the entry of default. Defendant admits that he was with the complaint "on or about after May 17, 2012." Cho Declaration, 3. Further, there is no factual basis to support mistake, inadvertence, surprise and/or excusable neglect. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. DIANE WARREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order That Matters In Req For Admissions Be Deem Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 21, 2013, Line 4: Plaintiff CACH, LLC'S Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deem Admitted Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is granted as unopposed. The matters in the request for admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions in the sum of $602.50 are awarded. Proposed Order submitted will be signed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to steve@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HELEN MICKIEWICZ VS. OPERA PLAZA MASTERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 21, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff HELEN MICKIEWICZ'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel multiple forms of discovery is denied except as further stated. The Court agrees all of the requests are either overbroad as to time and scope, vague, ambiguous, irrelevant, or impermissibly invade third parties' privacy interests. The surveillance video depicting the subject occurrence has been supplied. However, the defendant shall search the minutes of the meetings of the Master Owners for a period of ten years prior to the date of the subject accident, and if found produce all entries which in any way relate or refer to the design, maintenance, repair, incidents, or injuries occurring on the inner courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to steve@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION INC. VS. UNPARALLELED PRODUCTIONS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 21, 2013, Line 17, CROSS DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Cross-complainant has leave to amend to allege its contract damages more clearly. Paragraph 7 of the Cross-Complaint alleges the Cross-complainant did not make payment on the contract. Cross-complainant has leave to allege the fraud cause of action with more particularity. The demurrer to the negligence cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. That claim merely restates the breach of contract cause of action. Cross-complainant does not highlight a legal duty that cross-defendant breached that is independent of the contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN MOORE VS. RICHARD BURDEN TEED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 21, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Continued to Oct 25, 2013 per agreement all parties. =(302/MJM)
